summary: Patched resistor value 1k to 2k in both ROOT.DSN and ROOT.CDB. User reported project opened and displayed/properties showed 2k. Resaved output stable.
Patched resistor value in ROOT.DSN only. User reported Proteus opened but displayed/properties stayed 1k. ROOT.CDB won and DSN display normalized back.
Patched resistor value in ROOT.CDB only. User reported Proteus opened and displayed/properties showed 2k. ROOT.CDB is authoritative for resistor values.
Patched terminal label A to B minimally in ROOT.DSN. User reported project opened and terminal displayed/properties showed B.
Patched terminal A to B using known nearby diff bytes. User reported project opened and label showed B. Minimal patch D1 was sufficient.
Multi-resistor terminal networks showed ROOT.CDB identical across isolated, series, parallel, ladder, divider, copied branches when refs/values same. Topology lives in ROOT.DSN.
Mismatched DSN/CDB tests showed DSN controls visible existence, CDB controls resistor metadata, ROOT.CDB must exist, and missing CDB entries can be rebuilt from DSN in some cases.
